sources.name: AICPA & CIMA CPA Exam Blueprints resource page
updates: The AICPA resource page lists Uniform CPA Examination Blueprints effective January 1, 2026.
Each exam section has five testlets: the first two are MCQ testlets and the last three are task-based simulation testlets.

AICPA listed section item counts: AUD 39/39 MCQs plus 2/3/2 TBSs; FAR 25/25 MCQs plus 2/3/2 TBSs; REG 36/36 MCQs plus 2/3/3 TBSs; BAR 25/25 MCQs plus 2/3/2 TBSs; ISC 41/41 MCQs plus 1/3/2 TBSs; TCP 34/34 MCQs plus 2/3/2 TBSs.
Blueprints organize content by Area, Group, Topic, skill level, task statements, and reference material.

sources.name: Illinois Board of Examiners Uniform CPA Exam page
updates: Illinois candidates must pass AUD, FAR, REG, and one discipline section: BAR, ISC, or TCP.
Each section is four hours and must be passed with a minimum score of 75.
Beginning January 1, 2024, Illinois uses a 30-month window from the score release date of the first passed section.

sources.name: Illinois Board of Examiners Education Requirements page
updates: Illinois requires a degree from a Board-recognized accredited institution and at least 120 semester credit hours to qualify under the education framework.
Business courses can include business ethics, business law, business communication, information systems, statistics, economics, finance, management, and related business coursework.
The ILBOE accepts online coursework from Board-recognized accredited institutions when transcripted for credit.

sources.name: NASBA CPA Evolution announcement
updates: The three discipline sections are BAR, ISC, and TCP.
BAR emphasizes analysis of financial statements, financial information, data, technical accounting, and reporting.
ISC emphasizes IT audit/advisory, SOC engagements, and data management.
TCP emphasizes federal tax compliance, nonroutine transactions, tax planning, and personal financial planning.
